lesson.menu_desc: Communicating Across Levels -- Who Decides What -- 28 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 4 -- Navigating Hierarchy -- Communicating Across Levels
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Fabiana Michelly Silva | Aula 4 -- Navigating Hierarchy

listenings.text: Hi Fabiana, this is Klaus. I want to help you navigate our organization before the next review. Honestly, I wish I had explained our structure sooner. Here is how it works: I am your main point of contact, your counterpart on the supply side. Above me sits our operations director, who has full oversight of the APAC accounts. If an issue is urgent, our chain of command expects you to align with me first, and then I loop in the director. My peer in logistics, Anna, handles freight, so for shipping questions she is your liaison. I do not have the mandate to approve large credits on my own; that needs buy-in from the director. I wish I had more bandwidth this week, but I will delegate the smaller tasks to my team so nothing slips. Please build a direct rapport with Anna too. Call me back and I will introduce you to the right people.

Hi team, here is a quick update after my call with Klaus at TechFlow. I finally understand their hierarchy. Klaus is our counterpart, but he reports to an operations director who holds the real oversight. Looking back, I wish we had mapped their chain of command months ago; it would have saved us a lot of time. For now, our priority is to align with the right level. I have asked Klaus to loop in his director so we can get buy-in for a credit on the delayed order. I also want each of you to build rapport with your peer on their side. Maria, you are the natural liaison with their logistics team. I do not have the mandate to change the contract alone, so I will delegate the legal review and escalate the rest to our vice president. I wish I knew their director personally, but Klaus will introduce us. Let me know if you have any blockers.

extra_audio.text: Fabiana realizes she does not know TechFlow's hierarchy. Klaus explains the chain of command and introduces his director. Fabiana aligns with her counterpart on the next steps. The team builds rapport with their peers on the supply side. They secure buy-in from senior management for the solution.
